title: Kaoru as JesusEvangelion as Christianity
wikitext:
[]: Kaoru as Jesus;Evangelion as Christianity             Quote Post by BriarPatches » Thu Oct 16, 2008 12:40 am Evangelion didn't make sense to me until episode 24. Witnessing Kaoru die at the hand of man(Shinji), in front of the cross, is when I got my epiphany. Kaoru is Jesus. Once I realized this, I started to see all of the parallels of Evangelion and Christianity. After all, Evangelion does mean gospel. So I fully believe that Evangalion is the telling of the gospel of Christianity. I want to get some momentum going here, as I could go on for a bit, but I want some interaction as I'm new here. So here are some other things I believe: 2nd Impact is the "great flood", 3rd Impact is the "second coming" of Christ, the scene of Kaoru and Shinji at the bath represents Jesus washing his disciples' feet.

[]: Re: Kaoru as Jesus;Evangelion as Christianity             Quote Post by Dramamine » Thu Oct 16, 2008 12:51 am BriarPatches wrote: After all, Evangelion does mean gospel. So I fully believe that Evangalion is the telling of the gospel of Christianity.

[]:             Quote Post by BriarPatches » Thu Oct 16, 2008 2:21 am I want this thread to be more like looking at Eva in the context of Christianity. Do my suggestions fit? Do they work? I don't really care about what the makers say. I want to look at Eva by itself, without regards to its creators. Lots of works have been ultimately interpreted to have very different meanings than what the creators say that they intended. Let's not just dismiss the relation this fast. What do you think about some of my specific points?

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Thu Oct 16, 2008 4:49 am I think it works better in terms of EVA being the period of the revelation, with 2nd impact being the judgement of pure souls and all that happens after just the struggles of the living damned against the fate of their own making. Does that make Gendo the antichrist to Keel's beast? Is Ritsuko the whore of babylon? Lots of ponderings are possible... though ultimately doomed to meaninglessly, I expect.

[]: Re: Kaoru as Jesus;Evangelion as Christianity             Quote Post by pat457 » Thu Oct 16, 2008 5:52 am BriarPatches wrote: Evangelion didn't make sense to me until episode 24. Witnessing Kaoru die at the hand of man(Shinji), in front of the cross, is when I got my epiphany. Kaoru is Jesus. Once I realized this, I started to see all of the parallels of Evangelion and Christianity. After all, Evangelion does mean gospel. So I fully believe that Evangalion is the telling of the gospel of Christianity.

[]:             Quote Post by AyrYntake » Thu Oct 16, 2008 8:41 am Well, we know thus far that Kaworu is Adam in human form, alluding to Christ as the Second Adam, I suppose. But what would you make of the entire Lilith story, and the Angels--including Kaworu--coming to reunite with Adam?

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Thu Oct 16, 2008 8:55 am http://tvtropes.org/pmwiki/pmwiki.php/Main/WhatDoYouMeanItsNotSymbolic

[]:             Quote Post by CorporalChaos » Thu Oct 16, 2008 9:23 am Is Gendo Jesus now?

[]:             Quote Post by AyrYntake » Thu Oct 16, 2008 11:00 am Of course, just because it looks symbolic doesn't mean it is. But it's still fun once in a while to find things that are both symbolic and vaguely fit the plot, be they intended by the author or not.

[]:             Quote Post by HENRCAST » Thu Oct 16, 2008 1:24 pm AuraTwilight wrote: http://tvtropes.org/pmwiki/pmwiki.php/Main/WhatDoYouMeanItsNotSymbolic

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Thu Oct 16, 2008 1:28 pm There was one of these discussions that mentioned while Kaworu is considered being Christ-like, Rei is herself due to all the things she did... (Sacrificing herself to save Shinji in episode 23', giving up her physical body in EoE after Third Impact was rejected....) I think there's some truth to it with Rei, too. But like it was mentioned earlier in this thread, Christianity comparisons could be made for various cast members, not just Kaworu. Also, just to make sure this point is understood: "There are a lot of giant robot shows in Japan, and we did want our story to have a religious theme to help distinguish us. Because Christianity is an uncommon religion in Japan we thought it would be mysterious. None of the staff who worked on Eva are Christians. There is no actual Christian meaning to the show, we just thought the visual symbols of Christianity look cool. If we had known the show would get distributed in the US and Europe we might have rethought that choice." -Tsurumaki (Assistant Director, End of Evangelion; Air)

[]:             Quote Post by Super Chris » Thu Oct 16, 2008 8:14 pm Taking a look at it through my eyes as an agnostic. Yes, I know that it was never intended to be representative of anything in Christianity, and that most of the symbolism was chosen because it 'seemed cool', just like is sometimes done here with Buddhism. But. Not all of it, but a lot of it still does work in context. It's not unreasonable to believe that they did some of their homework on it before they stuck a lot of it in. Although I've always felt it was closer to Judaism than Christianity myself, with all the Kabbalah in there. Anyway. The way I've kind of seen it is that Shinji and Kaworu are both Christ figures in there. Kaworu died for your sins. Shinji died so everyone can be resurrected. (That may not have been his intention, but it was the implied result. - "Anyone can return to life, so long as they can imagine themselves in their hearts" or something like that.) And then a comparison I heard someone else say, "So if Shinji is Jesus, does that make Misato Mary Magdeline?"

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Thu Oct 16, 2008 8:17 pm Shinji died? If you mean when his response was 0 in EoE during 3rd Impact, he reformed his body after rejecting Instrumentality.... (This also happened in episode 20.)

[]:             Quote Post by Grand Duke of Yashima » Thu Oct 16, 2008 9:42 pm I think if there's a direct analogy to a Christ figure in EVA, it's probably Rei, not Kaworu. Kaworu strikes me more like the devil, himself a fallen angel, especially as he presents himself as a temptation to Shinji, one that he ultimately has to reject. Besides, Kaworu doesn't really rise from the dead, but Rei does. Also, regarding Rei's character, there's more to it than just a self-sacrificial nature. It's a basic tenant of Christianity that man is a fallen creature and in need of redemption, and that this condition is passed on by the flawed "seed" of mankind from generation to generation. Rei, because she not born of man but rather is created from Lillith, doesn't possess mankind's fallen nature, and has no "natural sin" per se. I know the quote from Tsuromaki, and, even knowing it, there's just too much symbolism in the show to simply say that EVA's Christianity is strictly there for aesthetics. One wonders if the others in GAINAX were so wrapped up in the production details that they didn't quite understand what Anno was doing.

[]:             Quote Post by Super Chris » Thu Oct 16, 2008 10:23 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: Shinji died? If you mean when his response was 0 in EoE during 3rd Impact, he reformed his body after rejecting Instrumentality.... (This also happened in episode 20.)

[]:             Quote Post by Silent Lightning » Fri Oct 17, 2008 2:40 am Shinji is quite probably the only person ever to have been tanged twice.

[]:             Quote Post by pat457 » Fri Oct 17, 2008 4:18 am Grand Duke of Yashima wrote: I think if there's a direct analogy to a Christ figure in EVA, it's probably Rei, not Kaworu. Kaworu strikes me more like the devil, himself a fallen angel, especially as he presents himself as a temptation to Shinji, one that he ultimately has to reject. Besides, Kaworu doesn't really rise from the dead, but Rei does. Also, regarding Rei's character, there's more to it than just a self-sacrificial nature. It's a basic tenant of Christianity that man is a fallen creature and in need of redemption, and that this condition is passed on by the flawed "seed" of mankind from generation to generation. Rei, because she not born of man but rather is created from Lillith, doesn't possess mankind's fallen nature, and has no "natural sin" per se. I know the quote from Tsuromaki, and, even knowing it, there's just too much symbolism in the show to simply say that EVA's Christianity is strictly there for aesthetics. One wonders if the others in GAINAX were so wrapped up in the production details that they didn't quite understand what Anno was doing.

[]:             Quote Post by AyrYntake » Sat Oct 18, 2008 9:43 am Come on! Shinji's been a Christ figure for ages , what with the crucifixion and being the "Son of God" and all.

[]:             Quote Post by pat457 » Sun Oct 19, 2008 8:55 am AyrYntake wrote: Come on! Shinji's been a Christ figure for ages , what with the crucifixion and being the "Son of God" and all.

[]:             Quote Post by master_lloyd » Sun Oct 19, 2008 1:03 pm Dude you spelt Kaworu wrong. It's Kwaru as that's how they pronounce it in the dub.

Besides, Kaworu doesn't really rise from the dead, but Rei does. Also, regarding Rei's character, there's more to it than just a self-sacrificial nature. It's a basic tenant of Christianity that man is a fallen creature and in need of redemption, and that this condition is passed on by the flawed "seed" of mankind from generation to generation. Rei, because she not born of man but rather is created from Lillith, doesn't possess mankind's fallen nature, and has no "natural sin" per se. I know the quote from Tsuromaki, and, even knowing it, there's just too much symbolism in the show to simply say that EVA's Christianity is strictly there for aesthetics. One wonders if the others in GAINAX were so wrapped up in the production details that they didn't quite understand what Anno was doing.
